name: GT-orderunit-def
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372 (1.2.1, order unit space and order norm), VERBATIM: "An element  $e \in A^+$  is called an order unit for A if for all  $a \in A$  there is  $\lambda > 0$  such that  $-\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e$ . ... In this case A has a norm given by  $||a|| = \inf\{\lambda > 0: -\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e\}$ ." Hence the order norm is a norm (triangle inequality, homogeneity); and for self-adjoint x, ||x||<=c iff -c*1 <= x <= c*1.
